creator: fernape@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Vladimir Druzenko from comment #2)
To keep the summary clean, especially when it includes symbols like arrows and such. When updating a port, or any software really, the important information is the target version, not the source.

I've been doing this for some time now. I also remove unnecessary words that make the summary verbose, like:

"foo/bar: update the port from version x.x to version y.y"

This becomes:

"foo/bar: update to y.y"
